title: Hypotension and muffled heart sounds in a 55-year-old
presentation: A 55-year-old male with a history of lung cancer presents with progressive shortness of breath and dizziness over 3 days. He appears anxious and diaphoretic. Vitals: BP 88/60, HR 128, RR 24, SpO2 94%, Temp 37.0C. Exam: muffled heart sounds, JVD, no peripheral edema, BP drops further on inspiration. CXR shows enlarged cardiac silhouette.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Beck's triad: hypotension + JVD + muffled heart sounds = cardiac tamponade. Pulsus paradoxus >10mmHg confirms. Bedside echo shows pericardial effusion with RV collapse. Pericardiocentesis is life-saving.
